differential_diagnoses: Urosepsis secondary to bladder malignancy and recent instrumentation
Type A lactic acidosis due to shock
5-oxoprolinuria (pyroglutamic acidosis) due to chronic acetaminophen use
Adrenal insufficiency (secondary to chronic corticosteroid use)
next_steps: Calculate anion gap and delta-delta to characterize metabolic acidosis
Obtain arterial blood gas with lactate, electrolytes, and glucose
Order serum beta-hydroxybutyrate, serum metformin level, and urine organic acid profile
Perform diagnostic paracentesis or urgent imaging to evaluate for localized occult infection
Obtain morning serum cortisol and ACTH levels
Review complete blood count and repeat serum creatinine to assess for acute kidney injury
Perform repeat bedside cardiac evaluation to monitor for right ventricular strain/failure
